explicit_text: The Dorwart family traces back to two brothers who came over very early in American history and settled in the Pennsylvania Dutch country. We were Huguenots from France originally. The name is rare, but there are now several Dorwarts in the country. My family has a long legacy, and I am Frederic Griffin Dorwart, Jr., carrying on that name.

explicit_text: This interview is being recorded in Old City Hall in Tulsa, Oklahoma, which is actually my law office. The building was constructed before 1959 and was derelict for several years until a Tulsa architect named Coleman and a lawyer rehabilitated it. I've owned the building since then, and the corner of 4th and Cincinnati where it stands has even been named Frederic Dorwart Way, thanks to my law partner Paul DeMuro.

explicit_text: My father, Frederic Griffin Dorwart, was born and raised in Pennsylvania where his father was an Episcopalian minister. He was a doctor known for his even-keeled personality and practiced medicine in Muskogee, Oklahoma after being persuaded by the Fite brothers, who were his classmates in medical school. He was also an Army Air Corps flight instructor during World War I and flew biplanes at Kelly Air Force Base in San Antonio.
